operating_state: Total revenue declined to $13.9 billion for six months ended June 30, 2023, from $19.0 billion in the prior year period, driven by lower worldwide crude oil prices averaging $73.91 per barrel versus $100.10 in 2022. Oil and gas segment income remained resilient despite commodity price headwinds, supported by higher domestic sales volumes from increased Permian Basin and Gulf of Mexico activity. Chemical segment earnings decreased significantly due to lower PVC pricing and reduced demand, partially offset by lower ethylene and energy costs.
cost_structure: Oil and gas operating expenses increased due to higher domestic workover and maintenance activity. Chemical and midstream cost of sales decreased reflecting lower input costs, while purchased commodities declined significantly due to lower prices on third-party crude purchases.
material_events: Occidental achieved investment grade ratings from both Moody's in March 2023 and Fitch in May 2023, upgrading from previous sub-investment grade status. The company repurchased 19.7 million common shares for $1.2 billion under the February 2023 program and completed $1.3 billion in preferred stock redemptions including premiums, with $8.8 billion face value remaining outstanding. Algeria production sharing contract extension became effective in Q2 2023.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil price volatility, with WTI averaging $74.98 for the six months versus $101.35 in the prior year. The company faces ongoing geopolitical risks from the Russia-Ukraine war and potential OPEC actions affecting global oil markets. Inflationary pressures continue to impact operational costs, though managed through operational efficiencies and proactive contract management.
forward_outlook: Management prioritizes maximizing cash flow through focused investments in short and medium-cycle projects to enhance current and future cash flows. The company intends to utilize operating cash flows for preserving and enhancing existing assets, continuing shareholder returns through sustainable dividends and active share buybacks, and opportunistically reducing financial leverage. Capital expenditures support development of core oil and gas assets and emerging low-carbon businesses.
uncertainty_profile: Zero Coupon bonds totaling approximately $344 million can be put to the company in October 2023, though management expects sufficient liquidity to meet this obligation. Environmental compliance costs are expected to rise under increasingly stringent regulations. Debt maturities include significant amounts in 2024 and 2025, with management expecting current cash and credit facilities to be sufficient for near-term obligations.
